ideas.title: I Ran On A Treadmill At 15% Incline Until My Legs Gave Out — I Made It 43 Minutes
ideas.one_line_premise: Set a treadmill to 15% incline at 9 mph and run until my legs physically stop working, narrating body sensations in real time.

concept.title: I Ran On A Treadmill At 15% Incline Until My Legs Gave Out — I Made It 43 Minutes
concept.logline: Set a treadmill to 15% incline at 9 mph and run until my legs physically stop working, narrating body sensations in real time.
concept.promise: You're watching me run at 15% incline until I fall — the question is how long.
concept.payoff: At 95% runtime: legs give out, body slides off the back of the treadmill, timer overlay freezes at 43:07.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies 20-30 minutes; payoff reveals 43:07 — audience expectation exceeded, specificity of the second locks over-delivery.

opening.first_frame: Action frame: my legs mid-stride on the treadmill at full incline, body angled forward, timer already running at 00:12.
opening.first_line: Go — 15 percent. Legs are going to quit before I do.
opening.opening_action: The camera is locked on my running feet for the first 3 seconds — no cutaway, just strides.

build_phases.beat: running, timer visible, premise named
build_phases.note: Action + concept inside first 10% (design_rules_v3 #6).

build_phases.beat: minutes 5-12: sweat, first breathing check, "my stomach is bigger"
build_phases.note: Sensory ramp. Utterances <10 words.

build_phases.beat: minutes 13-30: repeated failure-and-recovery — I grab the rail, release, keep running
build_phases.note: Monotonic rise in visible distress. No pauses >1s.

build_phases.beat: minute 38-42: legs shake visibly, form collapses, slow to 3 w/s narration
build_phases.note: Peak zone. Short sentence. Reaction → wide on the last fully upright step.

build_phases.beat: 43:07 — legs lock out, body slides off the belt; timer freezes; single sensation word
build_phases.note: Golden final 5%. END_RECOVERY payoff word lands +0.026.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: Legs lock out, I slide off the belt and collapse against the wall; the overlay freezes at 43:07; I say one word into the camera.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with sensation + impact: "stomach feels insane."

mid: alternate feet-pumping cuts with face reaction

duration_target.evidence: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

evidence: Hook[compound_frame_close_up_X_segment_setup_at_first_10s] via swipe_away_rate (principle princ_00034, n=65, csw=-0.148).
Structure[monotonic_rise]: Progression ↑↑↑ = 4.19M vs ↓↓↓ = 222K (19x gap). Every quarter beats the last.
Structure[late_peak_arc]: Retention pattern: videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median).
Structure[golden_final_5pct]: Videos at 95-99% percentile gain +8.0% above baseline vs -0.4% rest.
Structure[visceral_body_language]: Retention gain: painful +5.9%, curious +6.1%, numb +5.0%, stomach +5.1%. Sensory rate weight +1.59 in regression.
Structure[fast_pacing_no_pauses]: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Pauses >1s r=-0.22. Transitions ('so', 'however') drop -11.2% / -8.6%.
Duration[sweet_spot_46_60] 52s: 50-55s = 2.88M avg views. 40-55s safe range. 15-30s = death zone.

top_indicators.quantification: The gap between hook above_baseline and payoff above_baseline. NEGATIVE correlation = over-delivery wins. Videos where the ending exceeds the hook promise get 9x more views.
top_indicators.design_rule: The ending MUST exceed the opening promise. Never frontload your best content. Build to a climax that surpasses the hook.

top_indicators.quantification: Above-baseline retention at 80-95% of the video. The single strongest zone predictor. Q4 videos average +0.055 here while Q1 averages -0.089.
top_indicators.design_rule: Save the biggest payoff for the final 15%. The ending matters 8.5x more than the hook for total views.

top_indicators.quantification: Longest sustained run of above-baseline retention. Mean run = 50 points. % time above baseline also r=+0.47. Sustained quality > dramatic peaks.
top_indicators.design_rule: Consistency beats spikes. One long above-baseline run is worth more than multiple isolated peaks.

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

top_indicators.quantification: MORE retention peaks = FEWER views. Fewer, bigger dramatic moments > many small ones. Peak/drop ratio also inversely predicts (r=-0.33).
top_indicators.design_rule: Don't try to pack in multiple peaks. Build one clear narrative arc with one major payoff.

ideas.title: I Ate 20 Carolina Reapers In A Row — My Body Made Me Stop At 14
ideas.one_line_premise: Eat 20 Carolina Reaper peppers back-to-back on camera and narrate every sensation until my body physically refuses to swallow another one.

concept.title: I Ate 20 Carolina Reapers In A Row — My Body Made Me Stop At 14
concept.logline: Eat 20 Carolina Reaper peppers back-to-back on camera and narrate every sensation until my body physically refuses to swallow another one.
concept.promise: You're going to see 20 Carolina Reapers and a timer — the question is which pepper my body quits on.
concept.payoff: At 95% of runtime, pepper #14 goes in, comes back out; my body refuses; a timer overlay freezes at the exact second, and a single word appears.
concept.over_delivery_note: Setup implies I'll make it to 15-18; payoff lands at #14 with the exact second stamped. Hook_payoff_gap over-delivery by specificity.

opening.first_frame: Close-up: my hand holding pepper #1, a numbered row of 20 peppers lined up on the counter behind it. My jaw is already tensed. No overlay.
opening.first_line: Go — 20 in a row. My stomach is going to stop me.
opening.opening_action: I bite into pepper #1 immediately and swallow within the first 3 seconds, camera holds on my face.

build_phases.beat: pepper #1 bitten, premise named, timer starts
build_phases.note: Concept + physical action in first 10%. Body already reacting.

build_phases.beat: peppers #2-5 fast cuts, sweat starts, sensation narration
build_phases.note: Scene_change density high early. Sensory word ramp begins.

build_phases.beat: peppers #6-11, slowing rhythm, eyes watering, body complaints
build_phases.note: Utterances under 10 words. Pauses <1s. "my stomach is bigger now."

build_phases.beat: peppers #12-13 — slow to 3.0 w/s, visible full-body shiver, reaction shot
build_phases.note: Peak zone. Short sentence at peak. Reaction → wide on #13 going down.

build_phases.beat: pepper #14: in the mouth, out of the mouth; timer freezes; single word overlay
build_phases.note: Golden final 5%. Body quit moment + exact timestamp = payoff.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: Pepper #14 gets halfway down, my body rejects it visibly on camera, a timer overlay freezes at the exact second (e.g., "4:12 — 14 of 20"), I mouth one word.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: End on impact word: "that was insane."

ideas.title: I Read The Great Gatsby Out Loud In A 34° Ice Bath Until My Voice Gave Out
ideas.one_line_premise: I step into a 34° ice bath holding a paperback of The Great Gatsby, start reading aloud from page one, and keep going until my body shuts my voice down mid-sentence.

concept.title: I Read The Great Gatsby Out Loud In A 34° Ice Bath Until My Voice Gave Out
concept.logline: I step into a 34° ice bath holding a paperback of The Great Gatsby, start reading aloud from page one, and keep going until my body shuts my voice down mid-sentence.
concept.promise: Watch me hold a book in 34° water and try to keep reading — my body decides when the words stop.
concept.payoff: Final 5 seconds: voice breaks mid-sentence; the exact chapter and page I reached appears on screen with a single sensation word.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook implies I'll make it a chapter or two — payoff reveals the exact chapter+page the body quit (specificity = over-delivery).

opening.first_frame: Tight close-up of my hand holding the paperback half-submerged, water beading on the cover. My breath fogs the air above the bath edge. No text overlay.
opening.first_line: Okay — in four. My voice is going to stop before the chapter does.
opening.opening_action: My hand plunges the book to eye-level as my shoulders drop under the waterline and I start reading the first sentence aloud.

build_phases.beat: enter water, start reading page one aloud
build_phases.note: Premise stated in first 10%. Body already visible in water (design_rules_v3 #6).

build_phases.beat: jaw starts to shake, first skipped word, brief sensation check
build_phases.note: Divergence at 22% — lock in that body is reacting.

build_phases.beat: reading continues with intermittent sensation updates ("my stomach is numb", "I can't feel my skin")
build_phases.note: Sensory vocabulary ramp. Utterances stay under 10 words.

build_phases.beat: voice slows, pages stick to wet fingers, I slow to 3.0 w/s
build_phases.note: Peak zone 60-80%. Reaction shot on a sentence I can't finish.

build_phases.beat: voice breaks mid-sentence on a specific page; book closes; chapter + page number appears as overlay
build_phases.note: END_RECOVERY 80-95%. Emotion word in final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: I try to finish a single line of dialogue; the word comes out as three syllables, not a word; my mouth stops; the book slowly closes; a single chyron reads "stopped: Ch 3, p. 47" with one sensation word below.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with a feeling word + impact word: "stomach's numb — insane."

mid: cutaway to jaw / lips / hand on book
late: wide shot of tub + closed book
single chapter/page number overlay at the quit moment
avoid: naming the water temperature in words ("cold", "freezing")

top_indicators.quantification: Later drops are 2.15x steeper than early drops (-0.129 vs -0.060). And late drops are almost never recovered (3/472). Position × severity interaction.
top_indicators.design_rule: A drop at 70% is fatal. A drop at 20% can be recovered. Front-load any risky content.

ideas.title: I Hiked Four Miles On A Rocky Trail In Nothing But Socks — My Feet Told The Story
ideas.one_line_premise: Start a four-mile trail hike in socks only, narrate every sensation as the terrain changes, and end on a close-up reveal of the socks at the finish.

concept.title: I Hiked Four Miles On A Rocky Trail In Nothing But Socks — My Feet Told The Story
concept.logline: Start a four-mile trail hike in socks only, narrate every sensation as the terrain changes, and end on a close-up reveal of the socks at the finish.
concept.promise: Four miles, just socks, one trail — and my feet are going to tell you how it went.
concept.payoff: Final 5 seconds: I peel the sock back to reveal the foot; single overlay shows the mile count reached and one sensation word.
concept.over_delivery_note: Setup primes a "will I make four miles" question; payoff is that the sock itself is the transformation — the foot inside is proof.

opening.first_frame: Tight close-up of my socked feet at the trailhead marker, one foot half-raised to step onto gravel. Trail sign is in-frame but unreadable.
opening.first_line: How four miles feels in a sock — my skin's going to tell you.
opening.opening_action: My socked foot steps onto the first patch of gravel; camera holds on the foot for the first 3 seconds.

build_phases.beat: trailhead step, premise named, mile 0 overlay
build_phases.note: Concept + body inside first 10%.

build_phases.beat: mile 1 — first pebble hits, first sensation narration ("my foot feels bigger")
build_phases.note: Divergence lock-in at 22%. Body already altered.

build_phases.beat: miles 2-3: escalating terrain, visible limp develops, sensory updates
build_phases.note: Utterances short. No pauses >1s. Monotonic distress rise.

build_phases.beat: mile 3.5: near-stop moment, slow to 3 w/s, face reaction
build_phases.note: Peak zone. Short sentence. Reaction → wide on the final switchback.

build_phases.beat: mile 4 finish: sit down, peel sock back, close-up on the foot underneath + overlay
build_phases.note: Golden final 5%. END_RECOVERY. One sensation word at reveal.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: At the trail-end marker I sit down, pull the sock off with one hand, camera cuts to a close-up of the skin underneath; overlay reads "4.00 mi" + one sensation word.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close with sensation + impact: "skin is numb — insane."

mid: alternate terrain close-ups with face reactions
mile-count overlay only at beat moments
late: slow push-in on the last step

ideas.title: I Glued My Hand To A Wood Board With Gorilla Glue And Pulled It Off With My Teeth In 30 Minutes
ideas.one_line_premise: Pour Gorilla Glue on a pine board, press my hand into it until it sets, then pull the board off using only my teeth within 30 minutes.

concept.title: I Glued My Hand To A Wood Board With Gorilla Glue And Pulled It Off With My Teeth In 30 Minutes
concept.logline: Pour Gorilla Glue on a pine board, press my hand into it until it sets, then pull the board off using only my teeth within 30 minutes.
concept.promise: My right hand is stuck to a pine board — I have 30 minutes and only my teeth.
concept.payoff: Final 5 seconds: the board releases with a visible tear of skin, timer freezes at 28:41, single sensation word appears.
concept.over_delivery_note: Hook sets up "will it come off?"; payoff delivers the exact minute + the visible skin evidence (specificity beats vague success).

opening.first_frame: Tight close-up: my right palm pressed flat onto a pine board, glue still visible at the edges. Timer in-frame at 30:00.
opening.first_line: Okay — 30 minutes. Teeth only, no tools.
opening.opening_action: I try to lift the board with my free hand first; it won't budge; I bring my mouth to the edge in the first 3 seconds.

build_phases.beat: demonstration that the board is stuck, timer starts, premise named
build_phases.note: Action + premise in first 10%.

build_phases.beat: first bite-and-pull attempt, minor skin pull, first sensation narration
build_phases.note: Body already engaged. Divergence lock-in at 22%.

build_phases.beat: repeated bite-pull cycles, sweat on forehead, sensory updates ("my skin is bigger")
build_phases.note: Event density inverse — fewer big beats > many small.

build_phases.beat: nadir at ~85%: the board barely moves, I pause, jaw shakes, slow to 3 w/s
build_phases.note: best_after_worst: nadir before climax = 5x gap.

build_phases.beat: the board tears free at 28:41; visible skin-pull; timer freezes; one sensation word overlay
build_phases.note: Golden final 5%. END_RECOVERY payoff. Emotion word final 10% = +0.069.

climax_and_payoff.climax_hint: With 1:19 left the board finally releases with a sharp crack; the camera cuts to the palm, angry red where the wood was; timer overlay freezes at 28:41; I say one word.
climax_and_payoff.closing_line_hint: Close on sensation + impact: "skin's numb — insane."

mid: alternate jaw cutaways with palm-side cutaways
timer drops only at beat moments
late: slow push-in on the final bite
avoid: naming glue by brand in overlay
